content:
Today’s 9to5Toys Lunch Break is headlined by this 2TB 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro hitting its best price yet at $170 off alongside Apple’s wild 8TB M5 Max MacBook Pro at $400 off, and some nano-texture models $220 off. Amazon’s M3 iPad Air clearance deals are back at up to $250 off and we also have Apple’s 11-inch 1TB M5 iPad Pro at $150 off. Head below for a closer look.

New 2TB 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro hits best price yet at up to $170 off today

Just after featuring the base model 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro at $200 off on Amazon, it is now delivering its best price to date on the 14-inch model with 24GB of RAM and 2TB of storage at over $165 off. But you’ll want to head below for a chance to land this one for even less.

Amazon is now offering the 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro with 24GB of RAM and the 2TB storage capacity at $2,433.50 shipped. That’s just north of $165 off the list price on the Space Black model and the lowest price Amazon has ever sold it for – the silver model is still up closer to full price.

However, the entirely trusted and fully authorized Apple dealers at Expercom are offering this same machine for a touch less in both the Space Black and Silver colorways:

Expercom is now offering hundreds in savings on various higher-end 2026 MacBook Pro configurations, and this 14-inch 24GB/2TB model is now selling for $2,429 shipped – that’s $170 off the list price and the lowest we have tracked from a reputable source.

You can browse through more of the notable deals landing this week on the new 2026 M5 Pro and M5 Max MacBook Pro models at Expercom right here – the is as much as $400 off the list on the super high-end models.

  • 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB/1TB $2,049 (Reg. $2,199)
  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB/2TB $2,499 (Reg. $2,699) – new all-time low at $200 off
  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 48GB/1TB $2,899 (Reg. $3,099)
  • 14-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 36GB/2TB $3,449 (Reg. $3,599)
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 36GB/2TB $3,699 (Reg. $3,899)
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 48GB/2TB $4,200 (Reg. $4,399)

Apple’s ridiculous 8TB M5 Max MacBook Pro is a giant $400 off today, nano-texture models $220 off

Alongside a host of notable discounts on the new 2026 MacBook lineup at Amazon, including the M5 Pro and the M5 Air, authorized Apple dealer Expercom is landing today with some compelling offers of its own – the upgraded M5 Pro and M5 Max models are up to $400 off the list prices.

Expercom is a well known and trusted authorized Apple dealer that tends to step in once in a while with some seriously notable discounts on Apple gear, and especially on the pro-grade premium configs. Today is one of those days.

The dealer is serving up notable discounts on some M5 Pro and M5 Max configurations you can’t even buy at Amazon. While these models aren’t the most universally appealing, if you are looking for a seriously powerful 2026 machine or something with the upgraded nano-texture display, you certainly do not want to buy them from Apple with deals like these:

You will find some highlights on this landing page, but we have pulled some notable offers from the mix below as well:

  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB/1TB $2,649 (Reg. $2,849)
  • Nano-texture Display
  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 48GB/2TB $3,279 (Reg. $3,499)
  • Standard Display
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 64GB/2TB $4,339 (Reg. $4,599)
  • Standard Display
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 128GB/8TB $6,949 (Reg. $7,349)
  • Nano-texture Display

M3 iPad Air clearance deals now live via Amazon at up to $250 off

Deals across the brand new M4 iPad Air lineup continue this week at up to $100 off on the top end and closer to $50 off on the more affordable models. However, Amazon has re-stocked some of the clearance deals on the M3 iPad Air lineup we wanted to highlight today – various models are now at $200 off the list price with the 1TB model at up to $250 off. Details await below.

M3 iPad Air 11-inch clearance deals:

  • 11-inch M3 iPad Air Wi-Fi + Cell 256GB $649 (Reg. $849)
  • 11-inch M3 iPad Air Wi-Fi + Cell 512GB $849 (Reg. $1,049)
  • 11-inch M3 iPad Air Wi-Fi + Cell 1TB $999 (Reg. $1,249)

As you can see above, the best and most recommendable deals in the lineup are limited to the 11-inch configurations above. The 128GB model is completely out of stock now, while the 13-inch models are largely following suit.

Just about all of the offers above are matching the lowest prices we have tracked all-time on these Wi-Fi + Cell configs and make for a notable opportunity to land a relatively new iPad release at some of the lowest prices to date.

The new M4 is here, but I still think the M3 makes for a great option unless you absolutely must have the new M4 chip. There are some enhancements on the latest model for sure, but they looks nearly identical and the upgrades will be largely negligible for most users. For more details, hit up our feature piece: M4 iPad Air vs M3 iPad Air: Here’s everything new.

M4 iPad Air 11-inch

  • 11-inch M4 iPad Air 128GB $559 (Reg. $599)
  • 11-inch M4 iPad Air 256GB $641 (Reg. $699)
  • 11-inch M4 iPad Air 512GB $836 (Reg. $899)
  • 11-inch M4 iPad Air 1TB $1,016 (Reg. $1,099)

M4 iPad Air 13-inch

  • 13-inch M4 iPad Air 128GB $746 (Reg. $799)
  • 13-inch M4 iPad Air 256GB $836 (Reg. $899)
  • 13-inch M4 iPad Air 512GB $1,019 (Reg. $1,099)
  • 13-inch M4 iPad Air 1TB $1,199 (Reg. $1,299)

Apple’s 11-inch 1TB M5 iPad Pro is nearly $150 off right now, more

Today’s accessories and charging deals:

  • Logitech’s ultra-slim light-powered Apple Magic Keyboard alternative never needs to be charged, now at $80 (All-time low)
  • Don’t miss Lexar’s 4TB SL500 portable SSD while it’s down to its lowest since price hike (Now 44% off)
  • Just $22 today gets you this 67W Baseus charger with retractable USB-C cable to fast charge your iPhone 17 (47% off)
  • Originally $100 Anker MagSafe 3-in-1 Qi2 15W Desktop Charging Station is just $39 today (61% off)
  • Just $45 today gets you this Anker 4-in-1 charging kit with 45W USB-C charger, 67W 3-port car charger, more (55% off)
  • Affordably add this tactical nylon strap to your Apple Watch band collection for just $14.50

All 4 colors of Apple’s most affordable new 1TB M5 MacBook Air are back at $150 off today

As we mentioned yesterday, some of the M5 MacBook Air deals are quickly jumping back up in price as of the start of this week. While it seemed as though all of the more affordable 13-inch models were headed in that direction, Amazon is once again offering the mid-tier 16GB/1TB model in all four colors back at $150 off this morning.

Amazon is offering Apple’s new 2026 13-inch M5 MacBook Air with the 16GB of RAM and 1TB storage down at $1,149 shipped. This includes the Midnight, Silver, Starlight, and Sky Blue models at the time of writing.

While there is still a chance we see these configs start to head back closer to full price, much the same as the more affordable 16GB/512GB model deals that are all at higher prices now, we are indeed seeing the straight $150 price drop on all colors at the moment.

This is a regularly $1,299 config that is now back at the lowest prices we have tracked since release in early March of this year.

There are also some other lingering $150 price drops across the lineup for folks looking towards the 24GB variants or the larger 15-inch form-factors:

  • 13-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/1TB from $1,149 (Reg. $1,299)
  • 13-inch M5 MacBook Air 24GB/1TB from $1,349 (Reg. $1,499)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/512GB from $1,149 (Reg. $1,299)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/1TB from $1,349 (Reg. $1,499)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 24GB/1TB from $1,549 (Reg. $1,699)

CASETiFY drops all-new The Mandalorian and Grogu Apple gear accessories ahead of May the 4th
